name: Merrill Lynch Financial Advisor Ellen Chiang
description_short: Ellen Chiang is a First Vice President Wealth Management Advisor at Merrill Lynch in Midtown Manhattan, offering personalized financial planning and investment management services.
description_long:
Ellen Chiang has been a wealth management professional since 2006, currently serving as a First Vice President and Wealth Management Advisor at Merrill Lynch's One Bryant Park location in New York. She holds the CRPS certification and combines extensive corporate financial management experience—over 15 years as a high-tech industry financial officer—with specialized expertise in private wealth management. A UCLA Economics-Business graduate, she brings multilingual capabilities (English, Mandarin Chinese, and Taiwanese) to her client relationships.

Chiang offers comprehensive wealth management services including personal banking, lending, investment solutions, retirement and savings accounts, insurance products, and wealth planning strategies. Her practice leverages both Merrill Lynch investment solutions and Bank of America banking products to provide integrated financial services. She emphasizes customized planning around clients' life priorities rather than investment performance alone, with access to planning tools, market insights, and educational resources through Merrill's platforms.

Chiang distinguishes herself through a consultative approach that prioritizes understanding clients' life goals, family needs, and personal values before developing strategies. Her background serving diverse client types—corporate executives, entrepreneurs, business owners, CPAs, attorneys, private companies, and non-profit organizations—across the country demonstrates broad expertise. She is actively engaged in community service, serving as Chair of the Estate Planning Council of New York City's Diversity & Inclusion Committee and on the board of Woodcraft Rangers, a non-profit supporting K-12 arts education.

As an individual advisor within a large financial institution, clients gain access to institutional resources and established banking infrastructure, though investment performance and specific portfolio outcomes depend on market conditions and individual strategy execution. Her practice primarily serves clients with substantial assets requiring wealth management rather than those seeking basic banking services.
